sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Chemistry involves the understanding of the structure and reactivity of matter from atomic- and molecular-level perspectives. Degrees in Chemistry involve training in analytical, inorganic, organic, and physical chemistry. Elective courses are also available allowing students to pursue interests in materials, biological, and other advanced areas of chemistry.

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Chemistry involves the understanding of the structure and reactivity of matter from atomic- and molecular-level perspective. Degrees in Chemistry involve training in analytical, inorganic, organic, and physical chemistry. Elective courses are also available allowing students to pursue interests in materials, biological, and other advanced areas of chemistry.

requirementCueLines: * Arts and Humanities (A&H), Social Science (SSc).
* Arts and Humanities (A&H), Social Science (SSc). Students are expected to understand and complete all general education requirements as detailed in the General Catalog.

* Arts and Humanities (A&H), Social Science (SSc). They are general education requirements. Students are expected to understand and complete all general education requirements as detailed in the General Catalog.

** All interested students are strongly encouraged to pursue undergraduate research. Student can count research credits as general elective credits needed for the degree. Chem 299W and 49W9 can also meet the Writing "W" requirements.

**The calculus based series is recommended. Students taking the calculus based course can apply one credit toward the science elective requirement. Students taking the algebra-based course may count one credit of physics lab (Phys 117, 118, 119) as a science elective.
*Arts and Humanities (A&H) & Social Sciences (SSc).

*Credit not allowed for both Math 207/307 and AMATH 351 or for both Math 208/308 and AMATH 352 toward science elective requirement. Only one statistics course (BIOST 310, Q SCI 381, STAT 311) may count towards science electives.

*Credit not allowed for both Math 207/307 and AMATH 351 or for both Math 208/308 and AMATH 352 toward science elective requirement.Only one statistics course (BIOST 310, Q SCI 381, STAT 311) may count towards science electives.

*note clicking on a subject (EX. CHEM) will take you to the course catalog for that subject, hovering on a course number will tell you the title and clicking on a course number will take you to the MyPlan course description of the course, clicking on "choose one" will take you to the information about course sequences.
A “science” GPA of 2.5 is required. The “science" GPA is calculated from CHEM, MATH, PHYS and/or BIOC courses used to satisfy the non-ACS certified degree requirements.
A cumulative grade point average of 2.0 is required for all chemistry courses counted toward the degree.
A minimum cumulative GPA of 2.0 for required chemistry, biology, and biochemistry courses.
A minimum cumulative GPA of 2.50 is required in the BIOC 440, 441, 442 sequence.
A minimum grade of 2.0 and a cumulative major GPA of 2.50 are required for all CHEM, BIOL, & BIOC courses counted toward the major.
A minimum grade of 2.0 is necessary for each required chemistry course.
Accelerated: CHEM 143 (6), 153 (6)
Additional 400-level chemistry courses , not previously mentioned, taken for a numerical grade. The two parts of this requirement must total eleven credits.
Advanced Mathematics (choose one course)

Advanced Research : Up to 3 credits of advanced undergraduate research may count toward this requirement. Research conducted outside of Chemistry or Biochemistry must first be approved by one of the undergraduate advisers .

Advanced Research : Up to 9 credits of advanced undergraduate research may count toward this requirement. Research conducted outside of Chemistry or Biochemistry must first be approved by one of the undergraduate advisers .
Algebra-based: PHYS 114 (4), 115 (4), 116 (4)
All required courses must be taken for a decimal grade, unless only offered on a CR/NC basis.
AMATH 351 (3)
AMATH 352 (3)
An overall cumulative grade point average of 2.0 is required.
An overall cumulative grade point average of 2.5 is required.
An overall cumulative grade point average of 2.50 is also required.
B H 311

Beginning Spring quarter 2010, students enrolled in degree programs in biochemistry and chemistry must maintain both a cumulative GPA and individual-course grades consistent with requirements for their degree. Students pursuing B.S. degrees must maintain a minimum cumulative major GPA of 2.50 and minimum 2.0 for individual courses required for the major. Students pursuing a B.A. degree must maintain a minimum cumulative major GPA of 2.00 and minimum 1.7 for individual courses required for the major. Failure to maintain these GPA and grade standards results in the student being placed on departmental academic probation for one quarter, and dropped from the major if marked improvement in academic performance is not achieved. Students who experience extraordinary circumstances may petition for one or more additional probationary quarters.
BIOC 440 (4), 441 (4), 442 (4) - 2.0 min. each course and 2.5 GPA average across all 3 required.
Biochemistry BS degree requires 193 credits .

Biochemistry is the study of chemistry involving living organisms. Degrees in Biochemistry involve training in organic, inorganic, and physical chemistry as well as in biology, genetics, and biochemistry. Elective coursework is also available to pursue interests in a variety of advanced topics involving the chemistry of biological systems.
Biol 180 (5)

parsedRequirementCourses.title: AMATH 351
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceHeading: Additional 400 level science courses may be considered for science electives after consultation and a petition is submitted to the biochemistry advisers.: Credit not allowed for both Math 207/307 and AMATH 351 or for both Math 208/308 and AMATH 352 toward science elective requirement. Only one statistics course (BIOST 310, Q SCI 381, STAT 311) may count towards science electives.
